articles.title: ICE officers set to deploy to airports as delays mount, border czar Homan confirms
articles.summary: Border czar Tom Homan says ICE agents will help the Transportation Security Administration "move those lines" while also enforcing immigration law.

articles.title: Israel Thought It Could Spur Rebellion Inside Iran. That Hasn’t Happened.
articles.summary: President Trump’s hopes that an Israeli plan to ignite an internal uprising against Iran’s theocratic government could bring the war to a swift end have so far been dashed.

articles.title: Gas Prices Have Risen More Than 30% in Some States in Weeks Since Iran War Began
articles.summary: Across the South and Southwest, where price hikes have been the most severe, drivers have lamented how the increased costs have cut into their budgets.

articles.title: ICE to Help TSA at Airports Amid Partial Shutdown, Trump’s Border Czar Says
articles.summary: Tom Homan, President Trump’s chief border official, cast the operation largely as one to help ease long security lines that have been frustrating passengers at U.S. airports.

articles.title: Italy's far-right Prime Minister's political future at stake in referendum vote
articles.summary: Italian Prime Minister's close relationship with President Trump may weigh on a referendum on reforming Italy's judiciary.

articles.title: NATO allies prepare for a Russian attack in the far North
articles.summary: Despite tensions, U.S. and other NATO nations conduct exercises in the far North off Norway to prepare for Russian attack.

articles.title: Israel's war strategy involves high-level assassinations. But will it win the war?
articles.summary: The war between the US, Israel and Iran is intensifying. NPR's Adrian Ma speaks with Yossi Melman, who reports on Israeli intelligence, about the state of the conflict as it enters its fourth week.

articles.title: What a team of investigative reporters found out about Banksy's identity
articles.summary: A Reuters investigative team discovered intriguing details about Banksy's identity after seeing his art pop up in Ukraine.

articles.title: Israel’s Missile Defense Under Scrutiny After Iranian Attack
articles.summary: Two missiles landed hours apart, wreaking havoc in two towns near a heavily guarded nuclear site in the Negev Desert.

articles.title: Politics chat: Trump's mixed messages on the Iran war, the latest on DHS funding
articles.summary: We look at President Trump's mixed messages on the war with Iran, plus the latest on Department of Homeland Security funding, which Congress has frozen over his immigration enforcement policies.

articles.title: Mullin Explored Bipartisan Deal to Rein in Immigration Crackdown
articles.summary: The G.O.P. senator President Trump chose to lead the Department of Homeland Security privately discussed concessions the White House has repeatedly rejected.

articles.title: The Supreme Court Could Make it Harder to Vote by Mail in the Midterms
articles.summary: The Republican National Committee wants to toss ballots arriving after Election Day. Critics say thousands of votes — a majority cast by Democrats — are at stake.

articles.title: This lab that's determined to discover new drugs isn't where you might expect
articles.summary: A scientist from Zambia who loves — LOVES! — chemistry runs a lab in South Africa that is being hailed for "extraordinary" work.

articles.title: An orthopedic surgeon explains the hand injury that has many MLB players on the bench
articles.summary: Baseball hitters are on a quest for power. But that quest comes at a cost. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe speaks to orthopedic surgeon Dr. Thomas DiLiberti about baseball players suffering hamate injuries.

articles.title: She cared for her mother for 14 years. She says she'd do it all over again
articles.summary: Kathy Barnes-Lou cared for her mother for 14 years before her death. She learned that caregiving can bring life's purpose into focus, even as it grinds you down.

articles.title: Democrats who won big in last November's general election are grappling with reality
articles.summary: Some Democrats who were swept into office last November are grappling with the reality of governing. The new leader of Pennsylvania's Lehigh County says urgency is needed.

articles.title: The Iran war is impacting the global economy, and Asia is particularly vulnerable
articles.summary: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks with Martin Wolf, chief economics commentator for the Financial Times, about how the war on Iran is effecting the global economy.

articles.title: The effects of the Iran war on environmental and human health, according to an expert
articles.summary: As the war in Iran enters its fourth week, the costs are adding up. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to Doug Weir, with the Conflict and War Observatory, about impacts to human health and the environment.

articles.title: Trump threatens Iran's power plants as war enters fourth week with no end in sight
articles.summary: We have the latest on the U-S and Israeli war on Iran, where in the past 48 hours, Israel has struck one of Iran's nuclear facilities and Iran has responded with strikes in Israel.

articles.title: Trump threatens to 'obliterate' Iran's power plants as Iran strikes 2 Israeli cities
articles.summary: Iran launched missiles at two southern Israeli cities that lie close to the country's main nuclear research center, while President Trump gave Iran 48 hours to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Cortina d'Ampezzo mixes Olympic legacy with Alpine glamour
articles.summary: Cortina d'Ampezzo, the "Pearl of the Dolomites," is a blend of Olympic heritage with celebrity chic, fine dining and Alpine tradition, even as climate change and new tourism reshape the area.

articles.title: Beshear Criticizes Vance in Ohio, Stoking 2028 Presidential Tensions
articles.summary: Mr. Beshear, the Democratic governor of Kentucky, sharply criticized Mr. Vance as both angle for potential presidential runs.

articles.title: News Outlets Pressure Pentagon to Restore Access After Court Ruling
articles.summary: A federal judge ruled on Friday that key parts of the Pentagon’s press policy were unconstitutional.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
